info.name: Microsoft Azure RedisEnterpriseManagementClient
info.description: REST API for managing Redis Enterprise resources in Azure.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Providers Microsoft Cache Operations

request.description: Lists all of the available REST API operations of the Microsoft.Cache provider.

response.name: Success. The response describes the list of operations.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Providers Microsoft Cache Locations Location Operationsstatus Operationid

request.description: Gets the status of operation.

response.name: Success. The detailed status of the operation is in the response.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Providers Microsoft Cache Redisenterprise

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Cache Redisenterprise Clustername Databases Databasename Forceunlink

variable.description: (Required) The name of the RedisEnterprise cluster.
(Required) The name of the database.

request.description: Forcibly removes the link to the specified database resource.

response.name: Force unlink operation succeeded. The unlinked database must be deleted before it can be recreated and linked again.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Force unlink operation successfully enqueued; follow the Azure-AsyncOperation header to poll for final outcome.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Cache Redisenterprise Clustername Databases Databasename Flush

variable.description: (Required) The name of the RedisEnterprise cluster.
(Required) The name of the database.

request.description: Flushes all the keys in this database and also from its linked databases.

response.name: Flush operation succeeded. All the keys are cleared in this and all its linked databases.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Flush operation successfully enqueued; follow the Azure-AsyncOperation header to poll for final outcome.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

description.content: Location URI to poll for result
